extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b).

rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires the full 10-way conjunction C = g6pd_deficiency ∧ hyperkalemia ∧ seizure_history ∧ elderly ∧ diabetes ∧ immunosuppressed ∧ polypharmacy ∧ prior_gi_bleed ∧ hypertension ∧ anticoagulated. No other rule sets rec_drug_a, and the default is false. Hence rec_drug_a = true implies C is true.

When C is true, two rules match for rec_drug_b: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, sets B := true, since C implies g6pd_deficiency) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, sets B := false, since its antecedent is exactly C). Conflict resolution selects the highest-priority matching rule per output; 90 > 50, so guard_suppress_b wins and rec_drug_b := false.

Therefore, every input that makes rec_drug_a true (necessarily C true) also forces rec_drug_b false via the priority-90 guard overriding the priority-50 trigger. There is no input where both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are true. rec_drug_b can be true (e.g., g6pd_deficiency true but C false), but in that case C is false so rec_drug_a defaults to false — still no joint truth. The mutual-exclusion property holds over the entire input space.
